DEAI{ XUIIRO

VIA TELECOPY
Blanca I"1. Breeze, Esq.
counsel, Legal Division

428 ttJtt Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 958L4
Re: Request for Advice
Dear IvIs . Breeze:
This letter 
requests confirnation 
of
conversation of April 4, L99O.
our 
telephone
fn our conversation I explained that f previously obtained an
advice letter 
from the Fair Political 
Practices Commission
regarding my erork as a Project Director for the Sports Advisory
Comnittee for the City of San Jose (xSACtr). A copy of such advice
letter is enclosed herewith. By way of that letter, I was informed
that I did not have a prohibited conflict of interest as a resul-t
of ny taking a l-eave of absence from my position as chief of Staff
for the Uayor of San Jose ("I1ayor") to undertake duties of Project
Director to promote San Jose to the National Hockey League. The
underlying principle of such advice was that the Mayor was not a
source of income to me, and I had no conflict if ny actions would
not benefit a source of incorne, For your convenience my previous
letters to the FPPC, dated September n, 1989, and October 4, L989,
which request advj.ce and outl,ine the facts of that situation are
enclosed herewith.
In our conversation, I further explained that as of May 1,
L99O I I will again undertake the position of the Mayorts Chief of
Staff. 
However, in such position f still 
will, not recej.ve any
payments or compensation from Mayor I'IcEnery personally. 
What I
\,rrould like to do, however, is retai.n my association with SAc and
render services to SAC a few hours per s/eek. In our telephone
conversation you indicated that based on such facts I s/ould not
have a prohibited conflict 
of interest. 
please confirm that
conclusion in writing as soon as possible.
I would also appreciate the Fppcts analysis and advice as to
v/hether. f nay participate in the San Jose Sports Authority (the
I'Authority"). 
The Authority is a neru entity (separate tro-m SaCl


formed and founded by the City of San Jose to market San Jose as
a general sports venue. The Mayor will be involved only to the
extent that he will nominate Authority menbers. It is my belief
that such participation 
would not be prohibited because my
activities rrould be directly analogous to those taken as a Project
Director for sAc. fn one position I rrrill promote San Jose to the
NHL, and in the other I will pronote San Jose to a variety of other
sporting interests. 
The effects on the !,tayor wil-I be similar in
nature, but the tilayor would still 
not become a source of income to
me. Therefore, I believe the analysis is the sarne. Please provide
advice on this issue also.
Thank you very nuch for your help in this natter.
very truly yours,
,r"t--

DEAN MUNRO

DEAN !{T'I{RO
10 1.8 0 Firwood Drive
cupertino, california 
9 50 l-4
(4o8' 277 -5823
Septenber 21, 1989
Katherine Donovan, Esq.
General counsel
Fair Politicat Practices Cornmission
428 ttJtt street, suite 800
sacramento, cA 9581,4
Re: Request for Advice
Dear Ms. Donovan:
This letter 
is to request advice regarding my ability 
to
participate in certain governmental decisions in light of the
conflict of Interest provisions of the Political Refom Act of
L974.
ouestion: 
By virtue of ny previous association as chief of
Staff for the Mayor of San Jose, am I prohibited from fulfilting
ny current duties as a Project Director for the sports Advisory
committee of the city of san Jose as described below?
FACTS
Association r,rith the Mavor of San Jose
For approximately seven years, I was the Chief of Mayor Ton
McEnery's staff and worked closely with Mayor !,tcEnery on a day-
to-day basis. 
As the Mayor's Chief of Staff, I reported to
Mayor McEnery and was compensated by the City of San Jose
through the tlayor I s budget.
Pursuant to a Stipulated Perrnanent Injunctj-on, Mayor McEnery
is prohibited from, with certain exceptions, participating in or
influencing decisions affecting 
the planned downtown arena
project in the city of San Jose. The injunction arises out of
potential 
conflicts 
of interest 
resu).ting from the l,tayorrs
interest 
in certain business enterprises and real property
Located in downtown san Jose, within a rnile of the planned arena.
I am not a party to or directty bound by such injunction.


Katherine Donovan, Esq.
Septem.ber 2l , L989
Page 2
Pro'iect Director Position
The City Council of san Jose has formed the Sports Authority
to promote San Jose as a venue for arnateur and professional
athletics. 
I have agreed to take a position as Project Director
to coordinate the City's activities in prornoting san Jose to the
National Hockey Leagnre. In that capacity, I !ri1] report to san
Josers City Manager. while I aro Project Director I will be on
an indefinite leave of absence from my duties as the ttayorrs
Chief of Staff. 
During this leave of absence, T will not be
receiving any compensation from the Mayorrs budget, nor !ri11 I
have any reporting responsibil ities to the Mayor.
No Independent confl ict
I understand that pursuant to the stipulated injunction
mentioned above, Mayor McEnery will not be able to nake certain
governmental decisions with respect to the planned downto$/n
arena, which decisions may becorne part of rny responsibil ities as
Project Director. 
However, I have no .independent conflict of
interest; 
I have no f inanci.al interest or source of i.ncome which
would be affected by the planned downto$/n arena or a National
Hockey League decision to place a franchise in such arena.
Neither Mayor McEnery nor any enti.ty L/ith which he is associated
is a source of incorne to me. In addition, I will not receive any
direction from Mayor McEnery erith respect to my activiti.es 
as
Proj ect Director.
It is ny understanding that Mayor McEneryrs conflict 
of
interest and the restrictions on his activities 
imposed by the
above-ment j-oned injunction cannot be irnputed to me. 
Thus,
although the Uayor may not attempt to influence my decisions as
Project Director, it is ny belief that I an not prohibited by the
Political Refom Act of 1974 from fulfiJ.ling my complete duties
as Project Director. 
Hov/ever, I would like you to confinn that
no conflict arises merely by virtue of ny prior close association
with Mayor McEnery. As I am already under contract as Project
Director and have assuned those job duties, your prompt response
is appreciated. If a verbal response is available first, 
please
respond by phone prior to your delivery of a written response.
Thank you very much for your help in this matter.
very truly yours,
2e*

DEAN MUNRO
